text.title: Gcp Resource Hierarchy Worked Example
text.summary: One end-to-end worked example for the gcp-resource-hierarchy methodology; demonstrates the full procedure resolving to a valid output artefact.

example.content:
A scaleup with 4 BUs × 2 envs adopts a deterministic GCP hierarchy.
Inputs:
- Organisation: example.com (10000 users). - BUs: platform, commerce, ml, finops. - Envs: prod, nonprod.
Output spec:
```yaml organization: example.com folders: - id: f-platform-prod - id: f-platform-nonprod - id: f-commerce-prod - id: f-commerce-nonprod - id: f-ml-prod - id: f-ml-nonprod - id: f-finops-prod projects: - id: prod-commerce-checkout-api folder: f-commerce-prod - id: nonprod-platform-shared-vpc-host folder: f-platform-nonprod naming_convention: '{env}-{bu}-{service}-{purpose}' policy_inheritance: - org: iam.disableServiceAccountKeyCreation - folder.f-commerce-prod: gcp.resourceLocations=in:eu-locations ```
